new_bullets: when asked to review a COVID-19 vaccination status, you must perform a comprehensive search of the `Immunization` resource for all historical entries rather than assuming no records exist based on an initial search. You must explicitly state the date of the most recent vaccine found in the record and, if found, compare it to the 12-month threshold before concluding whether a new booster order is necessary.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must explicitly search for and list all active medication orders by querying the `MedicationRequest` resource for all active statuses. Do not assume no opioids are present based on a limited or empty initial search; you must iterate through the full list of active medications to identify any opioid agents before concluding that a naloxone order is unnecessary.

when asked "Check if patient S6539215 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order," you must first query for the existence of any active device or procedure orders related to a "urinary catheter" across all order statuses before concluding none exist. If found, you must explicitly retrieve the insertion date and time to calculate the duration and verify the absence of any associated removal or discontinuation orders before determining if a new service request is required.

when asked to calculate average heart rate over specific time intervals, you must query the `Observation` resource using the standard LOINC code `8867-4` and, if that returns no results, perform a broader search for all `vital-signs` category observations to ensure the data is not simply under a different code. You must explicitly list all heart rate values identified within the 6-hour and 12-hour windows before calculating the mean, rather than prematurely concluding that no data exists.
